title: Adversarial Perturbations Are Formed by Iteratively Learning Linear Combinations of the Right Singular Vectors
abstract: White-box targeted adversarial attacks reveal core vulnerabilities in Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), yet two key challenges persist: (i) How many target classes can be attacked simultaneously in a specified order, known as the ordered top-K attack problem (K ≥1)? (ii) How to compute the corresponding adversarial perturbations for a given benign image directly in the image space? We address both by showing that ordered top-K perturbations can be learned via iteratively optimizing linear combinations of the right singular vectors of the adversarial Jacobian (i.e., the logit-to-image Jacobian constrained by target ranking). These vectors span an orthogonal, informative subspace in the image domain. We introduce RisingAttacK, a novel Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP)-based method that exploits this structure. We propose a holistic figure-ofmerits (FoM) metric combining attack success rates (ASRs) and lp-norms (p = 1, 2, ∞). Extensive experiments on ImageNet-1k across six ordered top-K levels (K = 1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30) and four models (ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, ViT- B, DEiT-B) show RisingAttacK consistently surpasses the state-of-the-art QuadAttacK.
impact: This work advances the field of adversarial machine learning by introducing RisingAttacK. By improving the efficiency and scalability of ordered top-K adversarial attacks, particularly for large K values, this research highlights critical vulnerabilities in modern DNNs. However, adversarial attack methods also pose risks, as they may be misused to compromise real-world systems. For example, attacks on ranking-based systems could be exploited to manipulate search engine results or recommendation algorithms. To mitigate these risks, this work should be viewed as a tool for potentially strengthening defenses (e.g., as critics for them) rather than enabling malicious use. In addition, this work contributes to the broader exploration of optimization in machine learning by integrating techniques from traditional nonlinear programming into neural network-based problems. This direction holds promise for both adversarial research and other optimization tasks in machine learning, offering a foundation for solving increasingly complex challenges.

query_input.issue: White-box QuadAttacK perturbations cause targeted misclassification with low lp-norm distortion.
hazard: Adversarial perturbations crafted via ordered top-K attack using right singular vectors of the adversarial Jacobian enable highly effective white-box evasion of deep neural networks.
exposure: Model developers and auditors evaluating ViT-B robustness on ImageNet-1k benchmarks.

hipporag_result.vulnerability: ViT-B's adversarial Jacobian contains informative right singular vectors that enable iterative learning of small, multi-target perturbations in a low-dimensional image subspace.
hipporag_result.impact: Model auditors overestimate ViT-B robustness, leading to deployment of classifiers that adversaries reliably evade using ordered top-K attacks.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Adversarial perturbations crafted via ordered top-K attack using right singular vectors, through model auditor evaluations of ViT-B, trigger informative right singular vectors enabling small multi-target perturbations, leading to overestimated robustness and deployment of classifiers evadable by ordered top-K attacks.

dose_response: Highly effective white-box evasion via QuadAttacK enables cross-architecture blind spots, which in ViT-B benchmark evaluation leads to hidden vulnerabilities and filtering failures.
vulnerability: ViT-B benchmarks without cross-architecture attack testing create blind spots to adversarial failures.
impact: Deployment with hidden vulnerabilities causes filtering failures, letting harmful content bypass safeguards.
